title: analyze_joins_graph data payload
description: Corpus-wide manuscript-join graph queries. Two modes: `per-tablet` returns the direct join-neighborhood of a single tablet (fetched live from eBL /fragments/{id} and flattened from the nested joins[] groups), and `top-hosts` returns the corpus-wide top join-rich tablets from the cached joins-graph artifact (~/.cache/cuneiform-mcp/joins-graph.json, built by scripts/extract-joins-graph.mjs). Both modes attach period + primary genre from the fragment-metadata cache where available; missing metadata surfaces as null rather than failing.

mode.description: Which query mode was executed. `per-tablet` is selected when a tablet_id is supplied; `top-hosts` is the default when only list_top_hosts is set.

tablet_id.description: Echo of the queried tablet id. Present only in `per-tablet` mode.

join_neighborhood.description: Direct (1-hop) join neighbors of the queried tablet. Present only in `per-tablet` mode.

direct_joins.description: Flattened list of museum-number neighbors from eBL's joins[] groups, with the queried tablet itself removed and duplicates collapsed.

joins_count.description: Number of unique join neighbors returned (the length of direct_joins). Matches the FragmentMetadata.joins_count field minus the self-entry that eBL includes in its joins[] groups.

top_hosts.description: Top-K corpus-wide join-rich tablets from the cached joins-graph. Present only in `top-hosts` mode.

total_fragments_with_joins.description: Number of corpus fragments with at least one known join (per the joins-graph cache build).

total_join_edges.description: Sum of all joins_count values across the corpus — the raw edge count before de-duplication.

avg_joins_per_join_host.description: Mean joins_count over fragments that have ≥1 join (total_join_edges / total_fragments_with_joins, 2 decimal places).

genre.description: Primary (first) genre hierarchy string from the fragment-metadata cache, or null when the neighbor was never enriched.
